signals.reasoning: Existing ETH long remains a small bounded-risk management hold, and the shift from CLEAR to CAUTION is not enough by itself to force an exit while the live risk remains defined. The position is still manageable and cleaner than rotating into a new idea.
BTC is still consolidating after the prior squeeze and CAUTION is enough to cap conviction. That keeps it neutral from a management standpoint rather than a reason to rotate away from ETH.
SOL is moving with the same broad major-crypto tape and does not present a better management setup than the current ETH position. Hold only.
TST remains a noisy momentum name after its squeeze-style move, making fresh action lower quality under CAUTION. Not a better rotation than the existing ETH hold.
STBL can still look interesting mechanically, but it remains too unstable to outrank the live ETH position. Keep it on the watchlist rather than rotate into it.
PEOPLE still reads more like recovery noise than a clean fresh trend. It stays in the hold bucket while ETH remains the active managed exposure.
MERL is still in rebound mode without becoming a clearly superior opportunity. It does not justify rotating away from the current ETH position.
FOGO remains a thin high-beta name where execution quality can deteriorate quickly. Under CAUTION it is still not a cleaner management choice than ETH.
MAVIA can move sharply, but that volatility makes it a lower-quality switch from a small already-open ETH position. Better kept as watch-only.
LDO still has some constructive rebound structure, but not enough separation to justify leaving the existing ETH hold. CAUTION keeps it neutral.
FARTCOIN remains active and green, but this is still a noisy squeeze continuation that becomes harder to manage under CAUTION. No rotation away from ETH.
